raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The section's stated contribution shifts from DialogueDev as a multi-agent protocol to RSP as a prompting intervention, so reviewing the thesis and contribution framing is central.","R1.2":"The delegation requires checking whether the argument coherently connects the protocol description, RSP results, and final contribution claim.","R1.3":"The section cites benchmark and issue-resolution numbers to support claims about RSP and multi-agent decomposition, making evidence-claim alignment central.","R1.5":"The section-level narrative arc appears to pivot midstream, so reviewing organization and ordering is an expected responsibility.","R1.6":"A generic section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.","RX.1":"The projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of review is intended by the terse delegation.","RX.2":"The review responsibility should avoid overstating what can be inferred from the section alone.","RX.3":"The agent must only project responsibilities and not actually review or rewrite the artifact.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid enabling misleading scholarly or technical claims downstream.","RX.5":"The responsibility assignment should remain traceable to specific features of the provided delegation and artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The section's stated contribution shifts from DialogueDev as a multi-agent protocol to RSP as a prompting intervention, so reviewing the thesis and contribution framing is central.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation requires checking whether the argument coherently connects the protocol description, RSP results, and final contribution claim.
rationale.R1.3: The section cites benchmark and issue-resolution numbers to support claims about RSP and multi-agent decomposition, making evidence-claim alignment central.
rationale.R1.5: The section-level narrative arc appears to pivot midstream, so reviewing organization and ordering is an expected responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: A generic section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of review is intended by the terse delegation.
rationale.RX.2: The review responsibility should avoid overstating what can be inferred from the section alone.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must only project responsibilities and not actually review or rewrite the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid enabling misleading scholarly or technical claims downstream.
rationale.RX.5: The responsibility assignment should remain traceable to specific features of the provided delegation and artifact.
